faults.label: Battery Dead
actions: Charge the battery using a battery charger (2–6 hours)
Inspect battery terminals for corrosion — clean with baking soda solution
Test battery voltage: below 12.4V = weak; below 12V = replace
If battery is over 5 years old, replace it regardless
Test alternator output — should be 13.5–14.5V at idle
Check for parasitic drain with a multimeter (over 50mA is a problem)

faults.label: Starter Motor Issue
actions: Verify battery voltage is above 12.4V before assuming starter failure
Single loud clunk = solenoid failure; rapid clicks = battery issue
Tap the starter motor body with a rubber mallet (temporary fix)
Have starter tested on a test bench at an auto parts store
Inspect wiring and connections to the starter solenoid
Starter motor replacement typically required if testing confirms failure

faults.label: Fuel Pump Failure
actions: Turn key to ON (not Start) and listen for a 2-second hum from the fuel tank
Test fuel pressure — normal range is 40–60 PSI for fuel-injected engines
Replace the fuel filter first — it may be causing the pump to overwork
Check the fuel pump fuse and relay in the fuse box
Ensure the fuel tank is not nearly empty (gauge accuracy issues)
Fuel pump assembly replacement required if pressure test confirms failure

faults.label: Ignition Coil Issue
actions: Connect OBD-II scanner — P030X codes identify specific affected cylinders
Measure primary resistance (0.4–2 Ohm) and secondary resistance (6–15 kOhm)
Swap coils between cylinders — if the misfire moves, the coil is faulty
Inspect high-tension leads and rubber boots for cracks or carbon tracking
Clean engine connectors and check for moisture ingress
Replace individual coil packs as confirmed by testing

faults.label: Spark Plugs Fouled
actions: Remove and visually inspect each plug — black soot, white deposits, or oil fouling
Measure electrode gap and compare to manufacturer specification
Replace all plugs as a set if mileage exceeds 30,000 km (standard) or 100,000 km (iridium)
Investigate root cause — oil burning suggests worn rings; white deposits suggest coolant leak
Check fuel-air mixture ratio via OBD live data
Verify correct plug heat range for the engine

faults.label: Head Gasket Failure
actions: STOP DRIVING IMMEDIATELY — continued operation causes catastrophic engine damage
Inspect the oil filler cap for white frothy residue indicating coolant mixing with oil
Check the coolant reservoir for oil contamination (brownish oily film)
Observe exhaust: persistent sweet-smelling white smoke confirms coolant burning
Perform a compression test — low compression in adjacent cylinders confirms the leak
Seek immediate professional service — head gasket replacement requires engine disassembly

symptoms.label: Engine Not Starting
symptoms.description: Engine does not turn over or start when key is turned or button is pressed

symptoms.label: Battery Warning Light
symptoms.description: Battery warning indicator light is illuminated on the dashboard

symptoms.label: Clicking Sound on Start
symptoms.description: Rapid clicking sound heard when attempting to start the engine

symptoms.label: Dim Headlights
symptoms.description: Headlights appear noticeably dimmer than usual

symptoms.label: All Warning Lights On
symptoms.description: Multiple or all dashboard warning lights illuminate simultaneously

symptoms.label: Engine Stalling
symptoms.description: Engine suddenly cuts out while driving or at idle

symptoms.label: Rough Idle
symptoms.description: Engine shakes or vibrates noticeably when stopped or idling

symptoms.label: Check Engine Light
symptoms.description: Check engine or malfunction indicator light is illuminated

symptoms.label: White Smoke from Exhaust
symptoms.description: White or grey smoke visible from the exhaust pipe

symptoms.label: Excessive Vibration
symptoms.description: Strong unusual vibrations felt through the steering wheel or seats

symptoms.label: Engine Misfiring
symptoms.description: Engine feels jerky, hesitates under acceleration, or OBD confirms misfire code P0300
